title: We're getting there
body:
Spring feels right around the corner. The days are getting longer, and we're hitting the 60s and 70s every once in a while.

Now that the snow's gone, I've had a chance to take stock of how things did over the winter. For the most part, we're in pretty good shape. The perennial beds seem fine. Our arch nemesis, however, is as voracious as ever. The deer are relentless.

The arborvitaes I've been trying to grow as screening have been pruned back to twigs. I'm hoping once they get a little taller and out of reach, they'll have more room to run. Hopefully just a few more years. Those definitely took a hit.

The deer also went to town on the crocuses. I put netting up and everything, but somehow they got around it.Daffodils, though, they won't touch. Those are doing fine.

I managed to get the trumpet vine seeds in the ground. All the potted plants are looking pretty good, and I've got my eye on turning one or two of the junipers into bonsai. Starting a bonsai journey with them, at least.

Finally, the lilacs are just starting to come in, along with the forsythia. I think we're off to the races.

title: 2026 Garden Kickoff
body:
Alright, here's the plan everyone. We'll add to it as we go, but you've got to start somewhere.

Plans are useless, planning is essential.

# Vision

This summer, we want our garden to feel calm, open, and enjoyable.

- A simple, peaceful, and welcoming garden
- A spot where we can sit outside and watch airplanes fly overhead
- A good open view of the sky
- Heirloom tomatoes
- Herbs
- Maintain current perennial beds, trees, shrubs, and grasses
- An assortment of annual flowers. Foxgloves and Snapdragons for fun
- A trumpet flower on the trellis
- Start 1 or 2 more bonsai

Overall, we want the garden to be a place to relax, look up, and enjoy a mix of food, flowers, and a few longer-term projects.

_Pictured: Crocus and Daffodil bulbs starting to poke through_
